html:
Chestnut trees are trees found in the known world. Chestnuts can be used in cuisine.

Contents

  • 1 History
  • 2 Recent Events
    • 2.1 A Game of Thrones
    • 2.2 A Clash of Kings
    • 2.3 A Storm of Swords
    • 2.4 A Feast for Crows
    • 2.5 A Dance with Dragons
  • 3 Quotes
  • 4 References

History[ ]

Chestnut was the name of a horse owned by first Ser Arlan of Pennytree and then by Ser Duncan the Tall.[1] Ser Glendon Ball was sitting against a chestnut tree when he first met Duncan.[2] Roasted chestnuts were served during the wedding tourney at Whitewalls.[2]

Three-Finger Hobb once chopped up prunes, carrots, and chestnuts and cooked them in a hen.[3]

Theon Greyjoy found secret hiding places among the chestnuts, elms, and soldier pines of the godswood of Winterfell.[4]

Recent Events[ ]

A Game of Thrones[ ]

Bronn hones the edge of his sword beneath a chestnut tree prior to the battle on the Green Fork.[5]

A Clash of Kings[ ]

During the great ranging, Jon Snow sees a lightning-blasted chestnut tree overgrown with wild white roses in the haunted forest.[6]

Sansa Stark finds Dontos Hollard by a chestnut tree in the godswood of the Red Keep.[7]

A creamy chestnut soup is part of the meal enjoyed by Tyrion Lannister, acting Hand of the King, and Queen Regent Cersei Lannister at the Red Keep.[8]

A Storm of Swords[ ]

Ser Jaime Lannister sees stands of beech, oak, and chestnut on bluffs overlooking the Red Fork.[9]

While sacking a small sept, the Brave Companions hang a septon upside down from a spreading chestnut tree and use him for target practice.[10]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

Leo Tyrell has suckling pig in plum sauce, stuffed with chestnuts and white truffles, at the Checkered Hazard.[11]

Queen Margaery Tyrell and her retinue roast chestnuts in the kingswood.[12]

Stately chestnuts are among the trees near the crossroads inn from which the brotherhood without banners hang their victims.[13] While Brienne Tarth is being brought by the brotherhood to meet with Lady Stoneheart, Brienne sees a horse rooting for chestnuts.[14]

A Dance with Dragons[ ]

Bran Stark sees bare brown chestnut trees in the haunted forest.[15]

Illyrio Mopatis snacks on a bag of roasted chestnuts.[16]

Bags of chestnuts are among the stores of Castle Black.[17] Jon sees a chestnut tree turned into a heart tree by free folk while traveling to Mole's Town.[18]

Asha Greyjoy sees chestnut trees in the wolfswood.[19]

Venison with roasted chestnuts is included in the feast welcoming Ser Wylis Manderly back to White Harbor.[20]

A peddler offers hot roasted chestnuts during Cersei's walk of atonement through the streets of King's Landing.[21]

Quotes[ ]

The chestnut was leafless and skeletal, but its bare brown limbs were not empty. On a low branch overhanging the stream a raven sat hunched, its feathers ruffled up against the cold.[18]

—thoughts of Jon Snow
